name: mergetbapi
documentation:
# mergetbapi

This package contains Python bindings for the GRPC client interface to the MergeTB API

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.float_gt
documentation:
Field value of double strictly greater than this value.
 Note that this value can only take on a valid floating point
 value. Use together with float_epsilon if you need something more specific.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.float_lt
documentation:
Field value of double strictly smaller than this value.
 Note that this value can only take on a valid floating point
 value. Use together with float_epsilon if you need something more specific.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.float_epsilon
documentation:
Field value of double describing the epsilon within which
 any comparison should be considered to be true. For example,
 when using float_gt = 0.35, using a float_epsilon of 0.05
 would mean that any value above 0.30 is acceptable. It can be
 thought of as a {float_value_condition} +- {float_epsilon}.
 If unset, no correction for floating point inaccuracies in
 comparisons will be attempted.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.float_gte
documentation: Floating-point value compared to which the field content should be greater or equal.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.float_lte
documentation: Floating-point value compared to which the field content should be smaller or equal.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.string_not_empty
documentation: Used for string fields, requires the string to be not empty (i.e different from "").

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.repeated_count_min
documentation: Repeated field with at least this number of elements.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.repeated_count_max
documentation: Repeated field with at most this number of elements.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.length_gt
documentation: Field value of length greater than this value.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.length_lt
documentation: Field value of length smaller than this value.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.length_eq
documentation: Field value of length strictly equal to this value.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.is_in_enum
documentation: Requires that the value is in the enum.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.validator.FieldValidator.uuid_ver
documentation:
Ensures that a string value is in UUID format.
 uuid_ver specifies the valid UUID versions. Valid values are: 0-5.
 If uuid_ver is 0 all UUID versions are accepted.

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskRecordExistenceTypes
documentation:
What the task record's existence should be
 Using multiple of these on the same Task/ReconcilerManager/ReconcilerName
 works for Exists/Optional/Deleted, although your TaskForest will be funny.
 Ignore overrides everything else.

documentation: When the goal was first created.

documentation: The last time the goal was written.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskGoal.subkeys
documentation:
The etcd subkeys of the goal. Prefixes to these subkeys are determined by the GoalType.
 deprecated, as we just use Subgoals, Subtasks, and Subrecs instead

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskGoal.subgoals
documentation: Keys of subgoals

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskGoal.subtasks
documentation: Keys of subtasks (without the reconciler attached)

TaskGoal

 Goals are created at the highest level, usually by an apiserver handler
 Reconcilers should never create goals, they should only create subtasks

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskStatus

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskStatus.self_version
documentation: This is the value to set if you want to manually specify the status of a Task.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskStatus.duration
documentation:
Used to determine if the status is actually up to date
   and when a key is deleted while the reconciler is offline
   so we know what exactly to teardown.

 When a TaskStatus is used inside of a TaskTree, this is set to nil
   to save data and to not expose these to users.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskStatus.subtask_keys
documentation: as opposed to a key under which keys are put, i.e. /users/

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskStatus.current_status
documentation: Meant for human consumption. "Couldn't create /home/murphy".

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskStatus.task_revision
documentation:
Because the value of this depends on when the TaskStatus is read,
   this value will be overwritten when the TaskStatus is read.
 So, if you want to manually set the Status of a TaskStatus for use in etcd,
   DO NOT SET THIS VALUE, set LastStatus instead.

 The value of this depends on:
  - the version and value of the currently written task key
    - if they don't match, the status is "Pending"
  - if the reconciler doesn't seem to be alive, the status is "Unresponsive."
  - otherwise, this is identical to LastStatus
A TaskStatus is an individual step that changes the state of something to accomplish a goal.

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskMessage
documentation: A container for messages with a severity level

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskTree
documentation:
TaskTree is the tree of tasks spawned by an etcd key entry. It's a collection of task instances and
 task instances created by those, etc.
 This is ephermeral, i.e. not stored in minio or etcd and sent when requested.

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskForest
documentation:
Depending on the Goal Type, Task Forest either:
  - Wraps up all goals underneath if it is a supergoal
    - For example, all subgoals across a materialization
  - Wraps up all tasks underneath if it is a supertask
    - For example, all keys across all reconcilers when /users/murphy is created
 This is ephermeral, i.e. not stored in minio or etcd, and sent when requested.

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.TaskSummary
documentation:
This is a summary of a TaskForest
 Used when we want a status of something, but we don't care about the details
 Usually sent in addition to something, like a materialization
 This is ephermeral, i.e. not stored in minio or etcd, and sent when requested.

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.DeprStatus

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.DeprStatus.result
documentation: Status result

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.DeprStatus.worker

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.DeprStatus.info
documentation: Typically used for error or warning reporting form the worker.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.DeprStatus.version
documentation: Version of the state key this status is associated with.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.DeprStatus.reconciler
documentation: The reconciler who owns this status.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.DeprStatus.event
documentation: The event/type of status this is.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.DeprStatus.when
documentation: When the status was recorded

TODO: Depreciate this.
 This is currently here for backwards compatibility, so we can convert old statuses to new ones.

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.ReconcilerHealth
documentation:
ReconcilerHealth is something that a reconciler periodically checks in on and writes values to.
 This is used to see if a reconciler has gone rogue, but also for some basic performance metrics.

classes.name: mergetbapi.reconcile.ServerHeartbeat
documentation:
For reconcilers watching for server heartbeats, the ServerHeartbeat message indicates the
 expected interval between heartbeats

Specifically denotes a field as optional.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.google.api.FieldBehavior.REQUIRED
documentation:
Denotes a field as required.
 This indicates that the field **must** be provided as part of the request,
 and failure to do so will cause an error (usually `INVALID_ARGUMENT`).

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.google.api.FieldBehavior.OUTPUT_ONLY
documentation:
Denotes a field as output only.
 This indicates that the field is provided in responses, but including the
 field in a request does nothing (the server *must* ignore it and
 *must not* throw an error as a result of the field's presence).

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.google.api.FieldBehavior.INPUT_ONLY
documentation:
Denotes a field as input only.
 This indicates that the field is provided in requests, and the
 corresponding field is not included in output.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.google.api.FieldBehavior.IMMUTABLE
documentation:
Denotes a field as immutable.
 This indicates that the field may be set once in a request to create a
 resource, but may not be changed thereafter.

An indicator of the behavior of a given field (for example, that a field
 is required in requests, or given as output but ignored as input).
 This **does not** change the behavior in protocol buffers itself; it only
 denotes the behavior and may affect how API tooling handles the field.

 Note: This enum **may** receive new values in the future.

 the port). The host does not support path templating.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.Swagger.base_path

The base path on which the API is served, which is relative to the host. If
 it is not included, the API is served directly under the host. The value 
 MUST start with a leading slash (/). The basePath does not support path
 templating.
 Note that using `base_path` does not change the endpoint paths that are 
 generated in the resulting OpenAPI file. If you wish to use `base_path`
 with relatively generated OpenAPI paths, the `base_path` prefix must be 
 manually removed from your `google.api.http` paths and your code changed to 
 serve the API from the `base_path`.

classes.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.Schema

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.Schema.discriminator
documentation:
Adds support for polymorphism. The discriminator is the schema property
 name that is used to differentiate between other schema that inherit this
 schema. The property name used MUST be defined at this schema and it MUST
 be in the required property list. When used, the value MUST be the name of
 this schema or any schema that inherits it.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.Schema.read_only
documentation:
Relevant only for Schema "properties" definitions. Declares the property as
 "read only". This means that it MAY be sent as part of a response but MUST
 NOT be sent as part of the request. Properties marked as readOnly being
 true SHOULD NOT be in the required list of the defined schema. Default
 value is false.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.Schema.external_docs
documentation: Additional external documentation for this schema.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.Schema.example
documentation:
A free-form property to include an example of an instance for this schema in JSON.
 This is copied verbatim to the output.

`Schema` is a representation of OpenAPI v2 specification's Schema object.

 See: https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/3.0.0/versions/2.0.md#schemaObject

classes.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.JsonSchema

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.JsonSchema.ref
documentation:
Ref is used to define an external reference to include in the message.
 This could be a fully qualified proto message reference, and that type must
 be imported into the protofile. If no message is identified, the Ref will
 be used verbatim in the output.
 For example:
  `ref: ".google.protobuf.Timestamp"`.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.JsonSchema.title
documentation: The title of the schema.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.JsonSchema.description
documentation: A short description of the schema.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.JsonSchema.example
documentation:
A free-form property to include a JSON example of this field. This is copied
 verbatim to the output swagger.json. Quotes must be escaped.
 This property is the same for 2.0 and 3.0.0 https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/3.0.0/versions/3.0.0.md#schemaObject  https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/3.0.0/versions/2.0.md#schemaObject

classes.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityDefinitions

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityDefinitions.security
documentation:
A single security scheme definition, mapping a "name" to the scheme it
 defines.

`SecurityDefinitions` is a representation of OpenAPI v2 specification's
 Security Definitions object.

 See: https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/3.0.0/versions/2.0.md#securityDefinitionsObject

 A declaration of the security schemes available to be used in the
 specification. This does not enforce the security schemes on the operations
 and only serves to provide the relevant details for each scheme.

classes.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme.type
documentation:
The type of the security scheme. Valid values are "basic",
 "apiKey" or "oauth2".

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme.description
documentation: A short description for security scheme.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme.name
documentation:
The name of the header or query parameter to be used.
 Valid for apiKey.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme.in_
documentation:
The location of the API key. Valid values are "query" or
 "header".
 Valid for apiKey.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme.flow
documentation:
The flow used by the OAuth2 security scheme. Valid values are
 "implicit", "password", "application" or "accessCode".
 Valid for oauth2.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme.authorization_url
documentation:
The authorization URL to be used for this flow. This SHOULD be in
 the form of a URL.
 Valid for oauth2/implicit and oauth2/accessCode.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme.token_url
documentation:
The token URL to be used for this flow. This SHOULD be in the
 form of a URL.
 Valid for oauth2/password, oauth2/application and oauth2/accessCode.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityScheme.scopes
documentation:
The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.
 Valid for oauth2.

`SecurityScheme` is a representation of OpenAPI v2 specification's
 Security Scheme object.

 See: https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/3.0.0/versions/2.0.md#securitySchemeObject

 Allows the definition of a security scheme that can be used by the
 operations. Supported schemes are basic authentication, an API key (either as
 a header or as a query parameter) and OAuth2's common flows (implicit,
 password, application and access code).

classes.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityRequirement

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityRequirement.security_requirement
documentation:
Each name must correspond to a security scheme which is declared in
 the Security Definitions. If the security scheme is of type "oauth2",
 then the value is a list of scope names required for the execution.
 For other security scheme types, the array MUST be empty.

`SecurityRequirement` is a representation of OpenAPI v2 specification's
 Security Requirement object.

 See: https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/3.0.0/versions/2.0.md#securityRequirementObject

 Lists the required security schemes to execute this operation. The object can
 have multiple security schemes declared in it which are all required (that
 is, there is a logical AND between the schemes).

 The name used for each property MUST correspond to a security scheme
 declared in the Security Definitions.

classes.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.SecurityRequirementSecurityRequirementValue
documentation:
If the security scheme is of type "oauth2", then the value is a list of
 scope names required for the execution. For other security scheme types,
 the array MUST be empty.

classes.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.Scopes

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.grpc.gateway.protoc_gen_openapiv2.options.Scopes.scope
documentation:
Maps between a name of a scope to a short description of it (as the value
 of the property).

`Scopes` is a representation of OpenAPI v2 specification's Scopes object.

 See: https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/3.0.0/versions/2.0.md#scopesObject

 Lists the available scopes for an OAuth2 security scheme.

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.portal.v1.UserState.Active

class_variables.name: mergetbapi.portal.v1.ReservationDuration.duration
documentation:
How long to reserve the resources of the realization. After the duration
 ends the realization is available to be forcibly relinquished to free
 reserved resources.

 The format of the duration follows the golang time.ParseDuration with
 extra fields for weeks and days prepending the golang duration.

 Example: 2 weeks, 3 days, 4 hours, 5 seconds is "2w3d4h5s".

 Any field may be absent. "2w4h" is 2 weeks for hours.
 A day is 24 hours. A week is 7 days.

 Only used if "when" is "given".
